3.happening: Use the first string as the initial candidate prefix.
3.why: The longest possible common prefix cannot be longer than the first string.
3.invariant: prefix can shrink, but it never grows.

4.why: The candidate prefix must be valid for every string.
4.invariant: prefix is valid for all previously checked strings.

5.why: This string must start with the candidate prefix.
5.invariant: s is the string currently being validated.

6.happening: The current string did not start with the previous candidate prefix.
6.why: A mismatch means the candidate is too long or ends with an invalid character.
6.invariant: The true answer must be shorter than the failed candidate.

6.happening: Check whether the current string starts with the candidate prefix.
6.why: If it starts correctly, this string accepts the candidate.
6.invariant: starts_ok records whether the candidate works for this string.

7.happening: The candidate still fails, so the loop will shrink it.
7.why: Removing characters from the end is the only way to make a too-long prefix valid.
7.invariant: prefix is still a prefix of the first string.

7.happening: The candidate no longer needs shrinking for this string.
7.why: Either it matches the current string start or it is already empty.
7.invariant: The loop stops with a valid or empty prefix.

8.happening: Remove the last character from the candidate prefix.
8.why: The previous candidate failed for the current string.
8.invariant: The shortened prefix remains a prefix of the first string.

9.happening: The previous shortened candidate still did not match.
9.why: The algorithm may need to remove another character.
9.invariant: starts_ok will be refreshed for the current prefix.

9.happening: Recheck the shortened prefix against the current string.
9.why: After shrinking, the candidate may now match the start of this string.
9.invariant: starts_ok reflects the newest candidate prefix.

10.why: No non-empty candidate survived the comparisons.
10.invariant: The answer must be an empty string.

10.why: There may still be a valid common prefix.
10.invariant: The candidate remains worth checking against later strings.

11.happening: Store the empty string as the result.
11.why: No common prefix remains after shrinking.

12.why: Once the common prefix is empty, later strings cannot restore it.

13.happening: Store the surviving prefix as the result.
13.why: Every string has accepted this candidate after any needed shrinking.
13.invariant: result contains the longest common prefix.

14.invariant: The returned string is common to every input string.

init.why: The algorithm starts from the largest possible prefix.

loop.why: Each string must accept the current candidate.

check.happening: Check whether the candidate is valid.
check.why: A failed check tells the algorithm to shrink the candidate.
check.invariant: The candidate never grows after a failure.

update.why: The stored state changes only after a comparison outcome.
update.invariant: The candidate remains a prefix of the first string.

return.why: The longest common prefix is known.
